body:
![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The function `process_data` uses `unwrap()` on untrusted input from the network layer, which will panic on malformed packets. This is reachable from the public API endpoint `/api/v1/process`.

```suggestion
let data = parse_input(raw).map_err(|e| ApiError::BadRequest(e))?;
```

![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical-priority.svg)

Race condition: `session_map` is read without holding the lock in `cleanup_expired()` while `register_session()` can concurrently insert. This can cause use-after-free if the HashMap reallocates during iteration. The `Arc<Mutex<_>>` wrapper on the outer struct does not protect this inner access path.

This is a data race (undefined behavior in Rust terms if using unsafe, logical race with safe code).
Nice refactoring! The new structure is much cleaner. One small nit: the variable name `x` on line 15 could be more descriptive.
